text.title: Contractor Basics — Agent Rules
text.summary: Safety rules and prompt patterns for agents assisting contractor sourcing, screening, and ROI analysis.

rule.statement: Agent must not send a contract, sign an agreement, or make any payment commitment autonomously. Human approval is required at every commitment point (test task commissioning, contract signing, first invoice approval).
rule.rationale: Misclassification penalties ($1K-$25K per contractor), unenforceable contract terms from training data, and incorrect scope commitments are not LLM-recoverable errors.

rule.statement: When scoring applicants, output a ranked top-3 for human review and explicitly state "Do NOT recommend a hire — surface candidates for human decision". Never select the contractor.
rule.rationale: LLMs over-weight grammatical fluency in applicant summaries. For design and dev roles, portfolio quality (requiring human judgment) matters more than communication polish. The selection is a human call.

rule.statement: Before publishing any job post with a budget range, WebSearch current rates for the specific role and region (e.g. "UI designer Ukraine hourly rate 2025"). Never use hardcoded rate tables from training data.
rule.rationale: Market rates shift significantly by year and region. The illustrative rate tables in the source README are anchors, not current market data.

section.title: Prompt patterns

example.note: Applicant screening: "You are reviewing 12 Upwork applications for [role]. For each: extract years of experience, 3 most relevant portfolio links, communication quality (1-5), red flags, green flags. Output JSON array sorted by fit-score. Do NOT recommend a hire — surface a top-3 for human review."

ROI calculation: "Compute delegation ROI: my hourly value=$X, task takes me Y hrs/week, contractor rate=$Z/hr, contractor takes K hrs to do same task. Return weekly savings, annual savings, payback if onboarding takes M hrs."

section.reference: ROI calculator: my hourly rate, task hours/week, contractor rate, contractor hours input
Job post boilerplate with role, responsibilities, requirements, compensation, application instructions
